meaning: vt.放气,抽出空气,使缩小,紧缩(通货)vi.缩小
examples: I hate to deflate your ego, but you seem to have an exaggerated idea of your importance to me.
One sharp remark is enough to deflate her.
"When the stitches were put in, we started seeing the lung inflate and deflate, " Green said.
Things started to go wrong when Spain's frothy construction industry began to deflate last year.
